title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released photos of a suspect in an alleged armed robbery of a lemonade stand in South Boston.
The robbery involved two juveniles; one, a 14-year-old, is already in custody and facing multiple charges.
Authorities urge the public to assist in identifying the second suspect from the robbery on June 10.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering leaving the Lakers and is evaluating potential NBA teams for his final career move.
Rich Paul mentioned the Celtics as a possible destination for James, citing Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens.
Celtics' potential lineup with James includes Derrick White, Paul George, Mitchell Robinson, and Tatum.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticized the Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Parish compared the Brown trade to Dallas Mavericks' controversial trade of Luka Doncic in 2025.
He suggested Red Auerbach would be upset by the trade, important to Celtic history and legacy.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed gratitude and respect in a goodbye note to former teammate Jaylen Brown on social media.
Jaylen Brown was traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and multiple draft picks.
Brown felt disrespected by the Celtics' handling of the trade, leaving him surprised and critical of the process.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Neemias Queta has signed a four-year, $56 million extension with the Boston Celtics, running through the 2030-31 season.
The Celtics picked up Queta's team option for the 2026-27 season, valued at $2,667,944, before extending his contract.
Queta averaged 10.2 points and 8.4 rebounds in the 2025-26 season, finishing fourth in Most Improved Player voting.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates its 200th anniversary as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
Established in 1826, the restaurant is housed in a building likely built around 1716-1717 and is a National Historic Landmark.
The restaurant serves up to 1,500 guests daily and is known for its oysters and clam chowder, maintaining part of its original menu.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular, featuring Chance the Rapper and a drone show, takes place on July 4 at the Esplanade.
A World Cup quarterfinal match will be hosted at Boston Stadium on July 9, with significant traffic expected on Route 1.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail begins July 11, featuring over 60 tall ships in a five-day waterfront celebration for America's 250th anniversary.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House has hosted six U.S. presidents, including JFK, Obama, and FDR, over the decades.
Famous diners at the restaurant include Al Pacino, Meryl Streep, and Matt Damon, among many others.
Owner Joseph Milano commissioned a painting in 2003 depicting over 50 notable celebrity patrons.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year veteran of the New Hampshire State Police, died unexpectedly at age 41.
Ross served as commander of the Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit and was a devoted husband and father.
A fund has been established for Ross’ family, with a walk-through and services scheduled on July 6 and 7.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown expressed dissatisfaction with the Celtics' communication after being traded to the 76ers.
Brown was traded to Philadelphia in exchange for Paul George and multiple future draft picks.
Celtics' pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo influenced the trade move involving Jaylen Brown.
